data.content: No version conflicts — each package appears in only one project. I'll create the `Directory.Packages.props` and strip versions from the `.csproj` files.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.reasoningText:
Now I have all the information I need. Let me convert to Central Package Management:

1. Create a `Directory.Packages.props` file at the solution root with all unique packages and their highest versions
2. Remove `Version` attributes from all `PackageReference` elements in the `.csproj` files

Packages across all projects:
- System.Text.Json: 9.0.0 (Core)
- OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting: 1.15.0 (Web)
- Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi: 8.0.24 (Web)
- Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk: 17.12.0 (Tests)


- xunit: 2.9.3 (Tests)
- xunit.runner.visualstudio: 2.8.2 (Tests)

Since there are no version conflicts, I'll set up the central management file with all package versions defined centrally, then strip the version attributes from each project's package references while preserving other attributes like `PrivateAssets`.

arguments.file_text:
<Project>
  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>
  <ItemGroup>
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.AspNetCore.OpenApi" Version="8.0.24" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.12.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="OpenTelemetry.Extensions.Hosting" Version="1.15.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="9.0.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.9.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.2" />
  </ItemGroup>
</Project>

data.content:
Done. Here's what I did:

- **Created `Directory.Packages.props`** at the solution root with `ManagePackageVersionsCentrally` enabled and all 6 packages listed as `<PackageVersion>` entries.
- **Removed `Version` attributes** from all `<PackageReference>` elements in `Core.csproj`, `Web.csproj`, and `Tests.csproj`, preserving other attributes like `PrivateAssets="all"`.

No version conflicts existed — each package was referenced by only one project.
